transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, is this Daniel? This is Meera from Wati. How are you doing today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hey Meera, yeah this is Daniel. I'm good, thanks. I was actually expecting your call — we spoke briefly at the SaaS conference last week.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes! Great to reconnect. You mentioned your team was exploring WhatsApp for customer engagement — I've been looking forward to diving deeper into that. How's the post-conference chaos treating you?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Ha, you know how it is. Inbox is buried. But yeah, WhatsApp is top of mind for us right now. We're a D2C skincare brand, about 45 people, and we're growing fast in Southeast Asia.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's exciting — Southeast Asia is a huge WhatsApp market. Before I jump into anything about Wati, I'd love to understand what your current setup looks like. How are you handling customer communication today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So right now it's a mess, honestly. We have a shared WhatsApp Business phone that three people use. We also use Zendesk for email tickets. But the problem is WhatsApp is where 70% of our customers reach out, and we can't scale it with one phone number.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That makes sense — shared device WhatsApp is really common at your stage but it breaks down fast. When you say you can't scale, what does that actually look like day-to-day? Are messages getting missed, or is it more of a response time issue?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Both. We're averaging a 4-hour response time on WhatsApp, which is terrible for a brand that sells premium skincare. Customers expect fast replies. We've also had cases where two agents replied to the same customer with different answers. It's embarrassing.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Ouch, yeah — duplicate responses are a trust killer, especially in premium. And 4 hours in WhatsApp feels like an eternity when your competitors are replying in minutes. What about proactive outreach — are you doing any broadcast messaging or campaigns on WhatsApp?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: We've tried broadcasts for product launches but we're limited to 256 contacts per list with the basic app. And we have no way to track who opened what or clicked anything. It's basically sending into the void.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Right, that 256-contact ceiling is a hard wall. So let me make sure I'm understanding your situation correctly: you need a way to manage incoming WhatsApp conversations across multiple agents without collisions, bring your response time down significantly, and be able to run targeted broadcast campaigns with actual analytics. Is that a fair summary, or am I missing something?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's spot on. I'd also add automation — we answer the same 10 questions over and over. Order status, return policy, ingredient lists. If we could automate those, our team could focus on the conversations that actually need a human.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a great add. So here's why I think Wati would be a strong fit for you. We're built on the official WhatsApp Business API, which means you get a single verified business number that your whole team can work from — no more shared phones. Each conversation gets assigned to an agent, so there's zero collision. On the response time front, we have a no-code chatbot builder that handles exactly those repetitive questions — order status, return policies, FAQs — automatically. One of our customers, a D2C beauty brand similar to yours in Indonesia, brought their first-response time from 3 hours down to under 2 minutes after setting up their chatbot flows.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Under 2 minutes? That's impressive. What about the broadcasting side?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: With the API, there's no 256-contact limit. You can broadcast to your entire customer base — segmented by tags, purchase history, or any custom attribute you set up. And you get full delivery, read, and click-through analytics so you can actually measure campaign ROI. Our D2C customers typically see 45-60% open rates on WhatsApp campaigns, versus the 15-20% they were getting on email.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Those numbers are wild. Okay, one concern though — we're already paying for Zendesk. We can't really justify adding another tool unless it integrates or replaces something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Totally valid concern. Wati integrates natively with Zendesk — so WhatsApp conversations can sync as tickets if you want to keep Zendesk as your central hub. But honestly, a lot of our D2C customers in your size range end up consolidating into Wati entirely because it handles WhatsApp, chatbots, and basic CRM tagging out of the box. That's a decision you can make after you see it in action. We wouldn't push you to drop a tool until it makes sense.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's fair. What does pricing look like?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: For a team of about 5 agents — which sounds like where you'd start — you'd be looking at our Growth plan at $49 per month. That includes unlimited chatbot sessions, broadcasts, and the shared inbox. The API conversation charges from Meta are separate and usage-based, but for your volume I'd estimate around $100-150 per month on top. I can put together an exact estimate once I understand your monthly message volume better.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's actually way more reasonable than I expected. I was budgeting for something closer to Zendesk pricing.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We hear that a lot. So Daniel, here's what I'd suggest as a next step — let me set up a 30-minute live demo where I can show you the shared inbox, walk through a chatbot flow for your FAQ use case, and show you what a broadcast campaign looks like with analytics. I can also bring a solutions engineer who's worked with other D2C skincare brands. Would Thursday at 2 PM SGT work for you?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Thursday at 2 works perfectly. Can you also invite my marketing lead, Priya? She runs the campaigns side.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely — what's Priya's email? I'll send the invite to both of you.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's priya@glowskin.co.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. I'll send a calendar invite to both of you today with a brief agenda. In the meantime, I'll also send you a one-pager on how similar D2C brands are using Wati so you and Priya can review before the demo. Is email the best way to reach you, or do you prefer WhatsApp?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Email is great — daniel@glowskin.co.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Daniel, this was a great conversation. I'm genuinely excited about what Wati can do for GlowSkin — your use case is right in our sweet spot. Talk to you Thursday!

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Thanks Meera, looking forward to it. Have a good one.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You too, bye!
